message:
test(ffs-mvcc): add deterministic MVCC concurrency invariant tests

Encode core MVCC safety invariants as deterministic (non-flaky) tests
that construct specific interleavings rather than relying on thread
scheduling. Each test targets a distinct correctness property:

- Snapshot visibility chain: readers see only committed versions at
  or before their snapshot across a 5-version commit sequence
- Snapshot isolation: snapshots taken before any commits see nothing,
  even after later commits occur
- First-committer-wins (FCW): three concurrent writers to the same
  block — only the first to commit succeeds, others get Conflict
- FCW disjoint blocks: concurrent transactions writing different
  blocks both succeed without conflict
- No lost updates (serial): 20 serial commits to different blocks
  all observable at the final snapshot
- No lost updates (interleaved disjoint): interleaved begin/commit
  ordering for non-overlapping blocks preserves both writes
- Prune preserves latest visibility: pruning old versions does not
  break reads at the latest snapshot
- Read-your-own-writes: uncommitted staged writes visible within
  the same transaction
- Empty snapshot: current_snapshot on a fresh store returns a valid
  (but empty) snapshot with no visible data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
